title: Slow-cooker ham in cola
subtitle: Whole gammon poached in cola until completely tender
excerpt: A whole gammon joint submerged in cola and slow-cooked for six to eight hours until the meat is fully tender throughout. The cola tenderises and adds a slight caramel sweetness to the cooking liquid. Finish under the grill with a mustard and sugar glaze or serve unglazed with parsley sauce.
sourceNotes: Ham cooked in cola was popularised by Nigella Lawson in How to Eat (1998) and became a British Christmas tradition. The cola acts as both a tenderising and flavouring agent. The slow cooker version was a natural adaptation — the method requires gentle, submerged poaching for several hours, which is exactly what a slow cooker does on low. The gammon can be served warm or cold.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice and freeze. Excellent in sandwiches from frozen and thawed slices.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Can be cooked a day ahead and served cold. The flavour is excellent the next day.

content.text: The gammon needs to be fully submerged in liquid. If it sits above the cola, the top portion will cook differently from the rest. A tight-fitting lid is important — if the joint is too large for your slow cooker, use a smaller joint rather than trying to force it.

Place the {{gammon}} in the slow cooker with the {{onion}}, {{bay-leaves}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Pour over the {{cola}} — the joint should be mostly submerged. Top up with a little water if needed. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours, until the meat is tender throughout and reaches an internal temperature of 68°C.

Remove the gammon and rest for 20 minutes. If glazing, heat the oven grill to its highest setting. Peel away and discard the outer skin, leaving the fat layer intact. Score the fat in a diamond pattern with a sharp knife. Mix the {{english-mustard}} and {{light-brown-sugar}} together and spread over the fat. Place under the hot grill for 5 to 8 minutes, until caramelised and sticky. Watch closely — the sugar burns fast.

items.symptom: Gammon too salty even after cooking
items.cause: Joint was heavily cured and needed soaking
items.fix: For very salty gammon, soak overnight in cold water, changing the water once. Most supermarket gammon does not need this, but it varies between brands

items.symptom: Cola has reduced to a thick, very sweet syrup
items.cause: Lid was not sealing properly and steam escaped
items.fix: Check the lid sits flat. The cola level should not reduce significantly over 7 hours with the lid on

items.symptom: Glaze not caramelising under the grill
items.cause: Fat layer still wet from the cooking liquid
items.fix: Pat the fat dry with kitchen paper before applying the glaze. A dry surface caramelises faster

content.text: Ham in cola is a British Christmas and Boxing Day centrepiece. The slow cooker version removes the need to monitor a large pan on the hob for hours. Serve warm with parsley sauce or cold with piccalilli and crusty bread. Leftovers are excellent in sandwiches for several days.
